heading: Orphaned well site plugging, remediation, and restoration
label: 15907 Orphaned well site plugging, remediation, and restoration
notes: Amendments2021— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section related to orphaned, abandoned, or idled wells on Federal land.Pub. L. 117–582013—Subsec. (i). added subsec. (i).Pub. L. 113–40

children.heading: Indian Tribe
children.label: 3 Indian Tribe
children.text: The term “Indian Tribe” has the meaning given the term in .section 5304 of title 25

children.heading: Operator
children.label: 4 Operator
children.text: The term “operator”, with respect to an oil or gas operation, means any entity, including a lessee or operating rights owner, that has provided to a relevant authority a written statement that the entity is responsible for the oil or gas operation, or any portion of the operation.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: Not later than 60 days after , the Secretary shall establish a program to plug, remediate, and reclaim orphaned wells located on Federal land.November 15, 2021

children.label: i
children.text: identifying, characterizing, and inventorying orphaned wells and associated pipelines, facilities, and infrastructure on Federal land; and

children.label: i

children.label: ii
children.text: remediating and reclaiming well pads and facilities associated with orphaned wells;

children.label: iii
children.text: remediating soil and restoring native species habitat that has been degraded due to the presence of orphaned wells and associated pipelines, facilities, and infrastructure; and

children.label: iv
children.text: remediating land adjacent to orphaned wells and decommissioning or removing associated pipelines, facilities, and infrastructure;

children.label: C
children.text: provide a public accounting of the costs of plugging, remediation, and reclamation for each orphaned well;

children.label: D
children.text: seek to determine the identities of potentially responsible parties associated with the orphaned well (or a surety or guarantor of such a party), to the extent such information can be ascertained, and make efforts to obtain reimbursement for expenditures to the extent practicable;

children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), to administer a program to carry out any activities described in clauses (i) through (viii).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), a State shall not use more than 10 percent of the funds received under this subsection during a fiscal year for administrative costs under subparagraph (A)(ix).

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: The limitation under clause (i) shall not apply to funds used by a State as described in paragraph (3)(A)(ii).

children.label: I
children.text: an estimate of the number of jobs that will be created or saved through the activities proposed to be funded; and

children.label: cc
children.text: the State will use not less than 90 percent of the funding requested under this subsection to issue new contracts, amend existing contracts, or issue grants for plugging, remediation, and reclamation work by not later than 90 days after the date of receipt of the funds; and

children.label: bb

children.heading: Distribution
children.label: B Distribution
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations, the Secretary shall distribute funds to a State under this paragraph by not later than the date that is 30 days after the date on which the State submits to the Secretary the certification required under clause (i)(II) or (ii)(III) of subparagraph (A), as applicable.

children.heading: Deadline for expenditure
children.label: C Deadline for expenditure
children.text: A State that receives funds under this paragraph shall reimburse the Secretary in an amount equal to the amount of the funds that remain unobligated on the date that is 1 year after the date of receipt of the funds.

children.heading: Report
children.label: D Report
children.text: Not later than 15 months after the date on which a State receives funds under this paragraph, the State shall submit to the Secretary a report that describes the means by which the State used the funds in accordance with the certification submitted by the State under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a formula for the distribution to each State described in clause (ii) of funds under this paragraph.

children.heading: Description of States
children.label: ii Description of States
children.text: A State referred to in clause (i) is a State that, by not later than 45 days after , submits to the Secretary a notice of the intent of the State to submit an application under subparagraph (B), including a description of the factors described in clause (iii) with respect to the State.November 15, 2021

children.heading: Publication
children.label: iv Publication
children.text: Not later than 75 days after , the Secretary shall publish on a public website the amount that each State is eligible to receive under the formula under this subparagraph.November 15, 2021

children.heading: Distribution
children.label: C Distribution
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations, the Secretary shall distribute funds to a State under this paragraph by not later than the date that is 60 days after the date on which the State submits to the Secretary a completed application under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Deadline for expenditure
children.label: D Deadline for expenditure
children.text: A State that receives funds under this paragraph shall reimburse the Secretary in an amount equal to the amount of the funds that remain unobligated on the date that is 5 years after the date of receipt of the funds.

children.heading: Distribution
children.label: C Distribution
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations, the Secretary shall distribute funds to a State under this paragraph by not later than the date that is 60 days after the date on which the State submits to the Secretary a completed application under subparagraph (B).

children.label: i

children.label: I
children.text: The State has strengthened plugging standards and procedures designed to ensure that wells located in the State are plugged in an effective manner that protects groundwater and other natural resources, public health and safety, and the environment.

children.label: II
children.text: The State has made improvements to State programs designed to reduce future orphaned well burdens, such as financial assurance reform, alternative funding mechanisms for orphaned well programs, and reforms to programs relating to well transfer or temporary abandonment.

children.heading: Number
children.label: I Number
children.text: The Secretary may issue to a State under this subparagraph not more than 1 grant for each criterion described in subclause (I) or (II) of clause (i).

children.heading: Maximum amount
children.label: II Maximum amount
children.text: The amount of a single grant provided to a State under this subparagraph shall be not more than $20,000,000.

children.heading: Reimbursement for failure to maintain protections
children.label: iii Reimbursement for failure to maintain protections
children.text: A State that receives a grant under this subparagraph shall reimburse the Secretary in an amount equal to the amount of the grant in any case in which, during the 10-year period beginning on the date of receipt of the grant, the State enacts a law or regulation that, if in effect on the date of submission of the application under subparagraph (B), would have prevented the State from being eligible to receive the grant under clause (i).

children.label: cc
children.text: to decommission or remove associated pipelines, facilities, and infrastructure.

children.heading: Fiscal year
children.label: I Fiscal year
children.text: The Secretary may issue to a State under this subparagraph not more than 1 grant for each fiscal year.

children.heading: Total funds provided
children.label: II Total funds provided
children.text: The Secretary may provide to a State under this subparagraph a total amount equal to not more than $30,000,000 during the period of fiscal years 2022 through 2031.

children.heading: Tribal orphaned well site plugging, remediation, and restoration
children.label: d Tribal orphaned well site plugging, remediation, and restoration
A
children.text: provide to Indian Tribes grants in accordance with this subsection; or

children.label: B
children.text: on request of an Indian Tribe and in lieu of a grant under subparagraph (A), administer and carry out plugging, remediation, and reclamation activities in accordance with paragraph (7).

children.label: i
children.text: to plug, remediate, or reclaim an orphaned well on Tribal land;

children.label: ii
children.text: to remediate soil and restore native species habitat that has been degraded due to the presence of an orphaned well or associated pipelines, facilities, or infrastructure on Tribal land;

children.label: iii
children.text: to remediate Tribal land adjacent to orphaned wells and decommission or remove associated pipelines, facilities, and infrastructure;

children.label: iv
children.text: to provide an online public accounting of the cost of plugging, remediation, and reclamation for each orphaned well site on Tribal land;

children.label: v
children.text: to identify and characterize undocumented orphaned wells on Tribal land; and

children.label: vi
children.text: to develop or administer a Tribal program to carry out any activities described in clauses (i) through (v).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), an Indian Tribe shall not use more than 10 percent of the funds received under this subsection during a fiscal year for administrative costs under subparagraph (A)(vi).

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: The limitation under clause (i) shall not apply to any funds used to carry out an administrative action necessary for the development of a Tribal program described in subparagraph (A)(vi).

children.label: A
children.text: the unemployment rate of the Indian Tribe on the date on which the Indian Tribe submits an application under paragraph (4); and

children.label: B
children.text: the estimated number of orphaned wells on the Tribal land of the Indian Tribe.

children.label: i
children.text: the Tribal program for orphaned well plugging, remediation, and restoration, including legal authorities, processes used to identify and prioritize orphaned wells, procurement mechanisms, and other program elements demonstrating the readiness of the Indian Tribe to carry out the proposed activities, or plans to develop such a program; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the activities to be carried out with the grant, including an identification of the estimated health, safety, habitat, and environmental benefits of plugging, remediating, or reclaiming orphaned wells and remediating or reclaiming adjacent land; and

children.label: i
children.text: the number of orphaned wells that will be plugged, remediated, or reclaimed; and

children.heading: Distribution
children.label: 5 Distribution
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations, the Secretary shall distribute funds to an Indian Tribe under this subsection by not later than the date that is 60 days after the date on which the Indian Tribe submits to the Secretary a completed application under paragraph (4).

children.heading: Deadline for expenditure
children.label: 6 Deadline for expenditure
children.text: An Indian Tribe that receives funds under this subsection shall reimburse the Secretary in an amount equal to the amount of the funds that remain unobligated on the date that is 5 years after the date of receipt of the funds, except for cases in which the Secretary has granted the Indian Tribe an extended deadline for completion of the eligible activities after consultation.

children.heading: Delegation to Secretary in lieu of a grant
children.label: 7 Delegation to Secretary in lieu of a grant

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In lieu of a grant under this subsection, an Indian Tribe may submit to the Secretary a request for the Secretary to administer and carry out plugging, remediation, and reclamation activities relating to an orphaned well on behalf of the Indian Tribe.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: B Administration
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations under subsection (h)(1)(E), on submission of a request under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall administer or carry out plugging, remediation, and reclamation activities for an orphaned well on Tribal land.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: e Technical assistance
children.text: The Secretary of Energy, in cooperation with the Secretary and the Interstate Oil and Gas Compact Commission, shall provide technical assistance to the Federal land management agencies and oil and gas producing States and Indian Tribes to support practical and economical remedies for environmental problems caused by orphaned wells on Federal land, Tribal land, and State and private land, including the sharing of best practices in the management of oil and gas well inventories to ensure the availability of funds to plug, remediate, and restore oil and gas well sites on cessation of operation.
